channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: VIDEO: Couples embrace pets as symbolic wedding witnesses in Ecuador
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Ecuadorian couples are now including their beloved pets as symbolic witnesses in pet-friendly weddings, leaving paw prints on certificates. Discover this m
Read More: https://punchng.com/video-couples-embrace-pets-as-symbolic-wedding-witnesses-in-ecuador/

item.title: Lagos to sustain crackdown on illegal structures along Badagry Expressway — Commissioner
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Lagos State Commissioner announces sustained demolition of illegal structures and shanties along the Badagry Expressway to restore environmental sanity.
Read More: https://punchng.com/lagos-to-sustain-crackdown-on-illegal-structures-along-badagry-expressway-commissioner/

item.title: Ex-Man U striker Forlán to become Uruguay coach
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Uruguay's Football Association (AUF) is in advanced talks with Diego Forlán to become head coach of the national team on an interim basis.

item.title: ⚽ 'Ted Lasso' actor Fernández debuts in USL
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Fernández, a forward like Rojas, was subbed on in the 79th minute of El Paso's USL Cup match against New Mexico United on Saturday night.

item.title: Infantino: 64-team World Cup will be 'looked at'
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Gianni Infantino has confirmed FIFA will discuss expanding the men's World Cup to 64 teams after the 2026 tournament, saying every nation should have the chance to dream of playing at football's biggest event.

item.title: Sources: FIFA, FIFPRO in talks over heat protocol ...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: FIFA and global players' union FIFPRO are in discussions over establishing protocols to avoid games being played in excessive heat, sources have told ESPN.

item.title: Messi praises 'not normal' Argentina in WC defense...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Lionel Messi has said Argentina's remarkable run of success is "not normal" after the reigning world champions beat Switzerland to reach the World Cup semifinals, where they will face England.

item.title: Cubarsí calls for tolerance after ex-PM comments
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Spain defender Pau Cubarsí has called for tolerance after the nation's former Prime Minister, Mariano Rajoy, claimed that France "don't have any French players."

item.title: Haaland Sr: Norway 'robbed' in QF, 'well done ref'...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Alf-Inge Haaland claims Norway were "robbed" in their World Cup quarterfinal but hopes England go on to win the tournament after knocking out his homeland.

item.title: Norway boss fumes at 'clear' TV wire goal assist
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Norway boss Stale Solbakken has said he believes the overhead wires deserve an assist for England's equaliser.

item.title: Copy of Embolo red-carded for diving after VAR review
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Switzerland forward Breel Embolo was sent off in the second half of a World Cup quarterfinal match with Argentina on Saturday night after a video review showed he was guilty of simulation.

item.title: Man Utd in talks to sign Villa's Tielemans
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Manchester United are in talks to sign Youri Tielemans from Aston Villa.

item.title: Sky Sports pundits try to recreate Ghosh's incredible catch
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Sky Sports' pundits chatted with Richa Ghosh about her incredible catch against England and then try their best to recreate the moment.

item.title: The Most Expensive Software Bug isn’t in Your Technology; It’s in Your Leadership
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: Imagine if organisations upgraded their leadership as often as they upgraded their software. Security patches every month. Performance improvements every quarter. Some leaders would receive a notification that says: “Critical update available. Your leadership software is 15 years out of date.

Tielemans represented Belgium at the 2026 World Cup, where they were eliminated by Spain last weekend. The 29-year-old is no

item.title: I’m 67 with a $140,000 pension. Should I wait until 70 to claim Social Security so my wife gets more?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “When I pass, all my retirement income is reduced to $30,000 a year.”

item.title: Apple’s $600 Billion Rally Fueled by Traders Fleeing AI Selloff
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Investors are flocking back to Apple Inc. as nervousness about artificial intelligence spending weighs on the stocks of chipmakers and cloud-computing giants.

item.title: Oloja Chieftaincy Crisis: Court Orders Parties To Maintain Status Quo
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A Lagos State High Court has ordered all parties involved in the long-standing dispute over the Oloja of Lagos chieftaincy stool to maintain the status quo pending the resolution of the substantive case. It dismissed a preliminary objection challenging its jurisdiction. Justice Olalekan Oresanya iss

item.title: “FG’s Hike Of WAEC, NECO Fees From N27,000 To N50,000 Will Increase Out-Of-School Children” – Atiku
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: Atiku Abubakar, presidential candidate of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), says the federal government’s proposed N50,000 fee for Senior Secondary Certificate Examination (SSCE) will deny children from poor and middle-income families access to education. item.title: Lagos Task Force Warns Residents Against Blocking Roads For Social Events
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Lagos State Task Force has cautioned the residents against blocking roads for parties or religious events. It warned that those involved would face the full weight of the law. The agency, in a statement yesterday signed by its director, Press and Public Affairs, Gbadeyan Abdulraheem, said the Task F

item.title: Two Nigerians arrested over murder of compatriot in Ghana
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Two Nigerians, Daniel Victor Eze and David Oko Ewa, have been arrested by the police in Ghana over alleged murder of their compatriot, Nkemujika Uzorchuku Eze in Tamale. The post Two Nigerians arrested over murder of compatriot in Ghana appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Belgian Pro League club KRC Genk are interested in signing Nigerian striker Rafiu Durosinmi this summer, DAILY POST reports. item.title: NiMet Predicts Flash Flood In 25 States, FCT
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigerian Meteorological Agency (NiMet) has placed 25 states and the Federal Capital Territory (FCT) on high alert over the likelihood of flash floods between July 11 and July 20. In the North, the states on alert for flash floods are Bauchi, Jigawa, Taraba, Adamawa, Plateau, Benue, Nasarawa, Kwa

item.description: Cooking oil manufacturer Agro Processors & Atmospheric Gases Ltd. is planning an initial public offering in Pakistan this month to finance a plant expansion, adding to a record year for listings in the country.

item.title: CLO Fund’s 50% Tumble Fuels Power Struggle That Spills Into Open
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: A fight over who gets to run a $580 million credit fund has erupted into an unusually public Wall Street brawl, pitting claims of poor performance against accusations of a cash grab ahead of a proxy vote later this month.

item.title: Wall Street to Warsh: Skip the Guidance, But Tell Us What You Think About the Economy
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: In his zeal to avoid signaling where interest rates are headed, Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh has obscured something else that’s crucial to investors, analysts and other policymakers: How he would react when challenged by the economy.

item.title: US-Africa Week Ahead: Trump’s Africa envoy heads to West Africa as Washington courts Sahel juntas
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: Assistant Secretary of State Frank Garcia is travelling to Nigeria, Mali and Côte d’Ivoire for his inaugural trip to the continent, as the United States seeks to rebuild ties with the Alliance of Sahelian States.

item.title: Ex-Minister Nnaji pleads not guilty in forgery trial, gets N20m bail

item.description: Former minister Uche Nnaji pleads not guilty to certificate forgery charges and is granted N20 million bail by a Federal High Court in Abuja. The post Ex-Minister Nnaji pleads not guilty in forgery trial, gets N20m bail appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Standards change slowly until they change all at once. For most of online gambling’s history, players accepted verification delays, document uploads, and multi-day withdrawal timelines as the cost of participation. Nobody particularly liked it. item.title: How security forces cornered kidnappers and saved Orire pupils
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Officials said that experience reinforced the belief that, in some cases, carefully planned rescue operations based on reliable intelligence can be more effective than prolonged negotiations with heavily armed criminal groups.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Former Minister of Science and Technology, Uche Nnaji, was on Monday granted N20 million bail by the Federal High Court in Abuja after pleading not guilty to a six-count charge of alleged certificate forgery filed against him by the Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission

item.title: Ten people missing amid Spanish wildfires

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A former member of the House of Representatives, Hon. Ogbonna Nwuke, has said the decision of Governor Siminalayi Fubara to present the 2026 Appropriation Bill to the Rivers State House of Assembly, indicates a brighter future for the state. Nwuke, who stated this in a chat with LEADERSHIP in Port H

item.title: ‘It’s expensive’: Syria’s electricity has improved, but challenges remain
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Damascus residents say state-supplied power has noticeably improved recently but complain about the cost of energy.

item.title: 2026 World Cup: I feel it in my bones – Joe Cole predicts Argentina vs England semi-final
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former Chelsea playmaker, Joe Cole, is confident that England will beat Argentina in the semi-final of the 2026 World Cup. The Three Lions claimed a narrow 2-1 victory over Norway in the quarter-final, while Lionel Messi and co needed extra time to see off 10-man Switzerland. Cole, speaking on The R

item.title: Alleged Certificate Forgery: Ex-Minister Nnaji Pleads Not Guilty, Gets N20m Bail
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja, on Monday, granted N20 million bail to the former Minister of Science and Technology, Uche Nnaji, after he pleaded not guilty to a six-count certificate forgery charge the Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC) filed against him. T

item.title: 'Coalition of the willing' meets in Paris to bolster war support to Ukraine
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Ukrainian drone strikes killed four people and wounded seven in Russia on July 13, most in the Moscow region, as Kyiv upped its strikes in response to Kremlin's barrages. This comes as the 'coalition of the willing', a coalition of Ukraine's allies, gather in Paris. FRANCE 24's Gulliver Cragg tells

item.title: Explosions and large fires following Ukrainian attacks on Russia
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Explosions and large fires following Ukrainian attacks on Russia

item.title: FG Suspends Planned WAEC, NECO Fee Hike After Public Outcry
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Federal Government has suspended the proposed review of registration fees for the 2027 West African Senior School Certificate Examination (WASSCE) and the National Examinations Council (NECO) Senior School Certificate Examination (SSCE), following widespread concerns over the likely impact on st
